transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-08-10]
[00:00] You: Hiring pipeline review. We have 5 open roles and need to move faster on all of them.
[00:45] Speaker 1: Senior backend engineer — we have 3 candidates in final round. I'll complete all three technical interviews by August 14th and have a recommendation by August 17th.
[01:30] Speaker 2: Product designer — we've had slow candidate flow. I'll update the job description to be more specific about our stack and repost on Dribbble and LinkedIn by August 12th.
[02:10] Speaker 3: Data engineer — we just got 12 applications. I'll review all 12 applications and move qualified candidates to phone screens by August 13th.
[02:50] Speaker 4: For the head of marketing role, we're using an executive recruiter. I'll follow up with them and get a status update by August 11th.
[03:20] You: What about the junior frontend engineer role?
[03:35] Speaker 1: That's been open 6 weeks. The issue is we're seeing a lot of candidates who don't know TypeScript. I'll add a TypeScript screening question to the application form by August 11th.
[04:10] Speaker 2: I'll also set up a referral campaign internally — I'll email all staff asking for referrals with a $1,500 bounty for a successful hire. I'll send that email by August 12th.
[04:45] Speaker 3: We should also review our offer acceptance rate — we've lost 3 candidates to competing offers recently. I'll put together a compensation benchmarking report using Levels.fyi data by August 18th.
[05:20] Speaker 4: I'll update all job postings to highlight our remote-first culture and equity package — candidates have been asking about both. I'll have all 5 updated by August 13th.
[05:50] You: Great. Hiring target: all 5 roles filled by October 1st. Let's check in on this in two weeks.

Employee referral bounty set at $1,500 for successful hire
